'''Cedo Partu''' was a pioneer and early [[Colonist/Legends|settler]] of [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]],<ref name="CSWE" /> a [[City/Legends|town]]<ref name="Refresher 91">{{InsiderCite|91|Refresher Reading}}</ref> on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].<ref name="DB">{{DB|character|wuher|Wuher}}</ref> When Mos Eisley was a boomtown, Partu, a town leader, built a blockhouse that would serve as a fortification against [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]],<ref name="Refresher 91" /> a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] [[Homeworld/Legends|indigenous]] to Tatooine.<ref name="Tusken CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|268-269|Tusken Raider}}</ref> Generations later, the blockhouse would become [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina]].<ref name="CSWE" /> [[Sariss]], a [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] who went by the name<ref name="Sariss CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|127|Sariss}}</ref> Prophetess, once claimed that one of the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina's]] [[Bartender/Legends|bartenders]],<ref name="CSWE" /> the [[Human/Legends|Human]] male<ref name="DB" /> [[Wuher/Legends|Wuher]], was Partu's reincarnated [[Soul/Legends|soul]].<ref name="CSWE" />

==Behind the scenes==
The name "Cedo Partu" first appeared in [[1989]]'s ''[[Mos Eisley Cantina Adventure Set]]'' to identify the male bartender in Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina.<ref name="Mos Eisley">{{SWMB|set=Rebel Characters Adventure Set}}</ref> In later sources, however, the bartender was instead identified as "Wuher."<ref name="DB" /> In [[December]] [[2006]],<ref name="Insider date">{{SWArchive|url=community/fanclub/mag/news20061114.html|text=Inside ''Star Wars Insider'' #91}}</ref> [[Pablo Hidalgo]] [[Retroactive continuity|retroactively]] identified them as separate characters in "[[Refresher Reading]]," an article in [[Star Wars Insider 91|issue 91]] of the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine.<ref name="Refresher 91" />
